blockers.description: SR-4 execution caused 38 test file failures (68 tests) — massive regression from baseline 2 failures. Stashed all SR-4 changes to restore clean state at SR-3 commit e9cc3892.
blockers.workaround: git stash pop after diagnosing root cause

blockers.description: Import path updates via perl/sed were unreliable — double-quote vs single-quote matching failures caused 39 stale imports to persist across 26+ files. Manual Edit tool worked but is too slow for bulk operations.
blockers.workaround: Use Edit tool per-file or write a proper Node.js migration script

blockers.description: AGENTS.md in src/ explicitly forbids creating src/features/, src/config/, src/plugin/ without source-backed architecture decision. The restructuring plan exists but was not validated against this governance constraint before execution.
blockers.workaround: Update src/AGENTS.md to reference restructuring plan as the authorization, OR restructure differently

human_actions_pending.action: Decide whether to proceed with SR-4 as planned or revise approach
human_actions_pending.context: SR-4 bulk move broke tests badly. Need either: (a) smaller batch moves with test verification after each, (b) a proper migration script, or (c) revised restructuring plan that respects existing AGENTS.md governance

decisions.decision: Stashed SR-4 changes instead of committing broken state
decisions.rationale: 38 test file failures is unacceptable — would break the entire codebase

decisions.decision: SR-3 committed successfully before SR-4 attempt
decisions.rationale: SR-3 was clean: 13 files moved, 0 typecheck errors, 1816/1822 tests pass

next_action: Diagnose SR-4 test failures from stash, decide on revised approach
context_notes: SR-1 through SR-3 went cleanly with the pattern: batch move files → batch update imports → typecheck → test → commit. SR-4 broke because: (1) 12 module groups with internal cross-references are more complex than previous phases, (2) perl/sed regex for import replacement silently failed on quoting mismatches, (3) some 'errors' were actually pre-existing implicit-any issues masked by the volume of real import errors. The agent lost track of what was fixed vs not, ran typecheck with 0 import errors but didn't check tests before proceeding. Then test failures cascaded. Agent needs a fundamentally different approach for SR-4+ — either a scripted migration or much smaller batches with verification gates.
